name: Aeneas Extended
description: Aeneas, a Trojan hero, son of Prince Anchises and the goddess Venus (Aphrodite), is celebrated for his piety and leadership. He played a significant role in the Trojan War, fighting valiantly against the Greeks, though his destiny lay beyond the walls of Troy. After the city fell, Aeneas, guided by divine will, led a band of survivors on a perilous journey westward. This odyssey, fraught with storms, battles, and encounters with mythical creatures, took him across the Mediterranean Sea. He landed in Carthage, where he had a passionate affair with Queen Dido, but was compelled by the gods to abandon her and continue his quest. Aeneas eventually arrived in Italy, where he faced numerous challenges, including wars with the local tribes. Through perseverance and divine aid, he established a settlement that would eventually give rise to the Roman civilization. His descendants, Romulus and Remus, would found the city of Rome, fulfilling the prophecy that Aeneas would be the ancestor of a great empire. Aeneas embodies the virtues of duty, courage, and piety, and his story serves as a foundation myth for Roman identity, linking them to the glorious past of Troy and legitimizing their imperial ambitions. He is a symbol of resilience in the face of adversity and the enduring power of destiny. His journey represents the trials and tribulations necessary to build a lasting legacy. Aeneas's story is not just about military prowess, but also about the struggles of leadership, the burdens of destiny, and the sacrifices required to forge a new nation. His complex relationship with Dido highlights the conflict between personal desire and the demands of fate.
subtitle: Founder of the Roman People
achievements: Founding a New Trojan Settlement in Italy
Establishing the Lineage of Roman Rulers

keyMyths.title: The Sack of Troy
keyMyths.description: Aeneas escapes the burning city of Troy, carrying his father Anchises and leading his son Ascanius, fulfilling his destiny to found a new Troy in the West. This event marks the beginning of his long journey.

keyMyths.title: Aeneas and Dido
keyMyths.description: Aeneas lands in Carthage and falls in love with Queen Dido, but is forced by the gods to abandon her and continue his journey to Italy. This episode highlights the conflict between love and duty.

keyMyths.title: War in Italy
keyMyths.description: Aeneas arrives in Italy and faces opposition from Turnus and other local tribes, ultimately defeating them in battle and establishing a foothold for his people. This establishes the foundations for the future Roman empire.

sources.author: Virgil
sources.description: The primary source for the story of Aeneas, detailing his journey from Troy to Italy and his role in the founding of the Roman people.
sources.text: The primary source for the story of Aeneas, detailing his journey from Troy to Italy and his role in the founding of the Roman people.

sources.author: Homer
sources.description: Mentions Aeneas as a Trojan hero and son of Aphrodite, foreshadowing his importance in later narratives.
sources.text: Mentions Aeneas as a Trojan hero and son of Aphrodite, foreshadowing his importance in later narratives.
